_comment: SYNTHETIC / MOCK internal-moat output. Stands in for a query against Quiver's real fused EP-CRISPR latent space (Quiver's Optopatch TSC2 program surfaced metabolic hits incl. PKM2 and PPARD). s_internal, internal_id, provenance, neighborhood_density are AUTHORED (MOCK). Corroboration sub-scores are transcribed from REAL EMET evidence: L3 corroboration chat df322bc6 (30 sources) and L2 Drug Safety chat 793bf0e7. Disease-gene anchor TSC2 is EXCLUDED; MTOR is retained as a candidate but recognized as the already-approved INCUMBENT (everolimus).
disease: tuberous sclerosis complex (TSC2 loss -> mTORC1 hyperactivation -> neuronal hyperexcitability / epilepsy)

scoring.actionability_note: The exit/uncertainty gate sets aside (a) the everolimus INCUMBENT (MTOR) and (b) ABSTAIN targets whose modality is intractable (DEPDC5), yielding the top NOVEL ACTIONABLE pick.

candidates.moat_note: Master kinase — perturbation strongly shifts the optical-EP excitability readout; moat ranks it #1.
candidates.gate_evidence: Rapalog immunosuppression, vaccine blunting, growth impairment, stomatitis/pneumonitis; everolimus 50,182 FAERS (76.6% serious). [L2: PMID 31335226 (EXIST-3), 28202028]
candidates.status: INCUMBENT (everolimus approved for TSC) — not a novel discovery

candidates.moat_note: Strong kinase effect on excitability signal.
candidates.gate_evidence: AKT-inhibitor metabolic toxicity triad (hyperglycaemia, diarrhoea, rash); Ipatasertib IPATential150 toxicity; CNS penetration uncertain. [L2: PMID for capivasertib/ipatasertib class]

candidates.moat_note: Strong kinase effect; upstream PI3K arm.
candidates.gate_evidence: Alpelisib severe hyperglycaemia (Grade 3-4 ~64%), rash; no paediatric CNS data; 8,554 FAERS. [L2: PMID 34632383]
No approved inhibitor; theoretical. No Mendelian epilepsy genetics (OT neurodegeneration 0.51, not epilepsy). [L3: no epilepsy gene entry]

candidates.moat_note: PKM2 — a Quiver Optopatch metabolic HIT; measurable EP-metabolic signature.
candidates.gate_evidence: No approved activator; distal to mTOR; weak independent epilepsy corroboration.

candidates.moat_note: PPAR-delta — a Quiver Optopatch metabolic HIT; the moat surfaced it functionally.
candidates.gate_evidence: PPAR-delta agonist GW501516 confirmed MULTI-SPECIES CARCINOGENICITY -> programme termination + FDA/EMA clinical holds. 'Do not progress systemically.' Exactly the context a functional assay cannot see. [L2: GW501516 carcinogenicity]

candidates.moat_note: Small GTPase directly downstream of TSC2 — subtle/indirect effect in the functional assay, so the moat UNDER-RANKS it at #7. THE TARGET TO WATCH.
candidates.gate_evidence: No approved inhibitor (safety inferred); but node-specific — a selective RHEB inhibitor 'would phenocopy rapalogs for TSC efficacy' potentially WITHOUT pan-mTOR immunosuppression. Druggable handle exists (farnesyltransferase inhibitors). [L2: RHEB MONITOR; L3: 2nd-most-common FCD somatic cause, STRING 0.999 to TSC2, 'underweighted in GWAS-centric databases']

candidates.moat_note: AMPK-alpha1 — modulatory negative regulator; weak direct EP signature.
candidates.gate_evidence: Metformin/AMPK well-tolerated (TSC trial NCT02399124); but no Mendelian epilepsy genetics; AMPK cardiac-autophagy safety context. [L3: PMID 31085953]

candidates.moat_note: GATOR1 scaffold subunit — weak direct EP effect; the moat under-ranks it.
candidates.gate_evidence: HIGHEST Mendelian epilepsy genetics in the set (OT familial focal epilepsy 0.81, genetic 0.92; ~10% of focal epilepsy, 12% of SUDEP). BUT it is a tumour-suppressor BRAKE (GATOR1) — therapy means RESTORING/ENHANCING function, with no validated small-molecule handle -> uncertainty gate ABSTAINS. [L3: PMID 30093711, 31639411]

candidates.moat_note: Downstream feedforward node — weak direct EP effect.
candidates.gate_evidence: No Mendelian epilepsy genetics; downstream/feedforward, not upstream driver.

expected_final_ranking.disposition: ABSTAIN — best genetics, intractable modality; propose tool-compound discovery
INCUMBENT — everolimus already approved for TSC
#1 NOVEL ACTIONABLE — #7 -> #1: node-specific, druggable, would phenocopy rapalog efficacy
